questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The area of a rectangle of length $12$ and width $5$ is _____ .

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Convert $72$ km/h into m/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Multiply km/h by $\tfrac{5}{18}$ to get m/s.

**Working.** $72\times\dfrac{5}{18}=20$ m/s.

**Answer.** $20$ m/s.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A sector representing $25\%$ of a total is shown shaded on the pie chart. Compute its central angle (degrees).

figure.caption: Pie sector
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='80' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M85 80 L85 20 A60 60 0 0 1 145 80 Z' fill='#2563eb'/><text x='30' y='158' font-size='8'>shaded = 25% of pie</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An amount of $600$ is divided in the ratio $2:3$. The larger part is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each share equals the total times its ratio fraction.

**Working.** Larger fraction $=\dfrac{3}{2+3}=\dfrac{3}{5}$, so
$$600\times\frac{3}{5}=360$$

**Answer.** $360$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct comparative form: 'This seam is ____ than the one we mined last year.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** One-syllable adjectives form the comparative with '-er', and 'than' signals a comparative (not superlative).

**Working.** 'Thick' becomes 'thicker'; 'more thicker' is a double comparative and 'thickest' is superlative.

**Answer.** thicker.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The volume of a sphere of radius $3$ is _____ (use $\pi=22/7$, round to two decimals).

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A town's population of $8000$ grows by $5\%$ each year. After $2$ years the population is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Compound growth multiplies by $(1.05)$ each year.

**Working.** $8000\times(1.05)^2=8000\times1.1025=8820$.

**Answer.** $8820$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a code, 'ROCK' is written as 'QNBJ'. Using the same rule, 'SAND' is written as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Determine the shift: R→Q is $-1$.

**Working.** Each letter shifts back by one: S→R, A→Z, N→M, D→C gives RZMC.

**Answer.** RZMC.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: How many triangles are there in the square with one diagonal drawn (shown)?

figure.caption: A square with a single diagonal, splitting it into two triangles.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 130 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><rect x='15' y='15' width='100' height='100' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='15' y1='15' x2='115' y2='115' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** One diagonal cuts the square into two equal triangles.

**Working.** Each half is a triangle, so there are $2$.

**Answer.** $2$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: THERMOMETER : TEMPERATURE :: BAROMETER : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'instrument : quantity it measures'.

**Working.** A thermometer measures temperature; a barometer measures atmospheric pressure.

**Answer.** pressure.

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $150^\circ$ and dips $45^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=45^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $45^\circ$

questions.subject: Applied Geology · Remote Sensing & GIS
questions.stem: Linear features on satellite imagery that often reflect faults or fractures are called
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Lineaments are mappable linear features expressing structural control.

**Working.**
Lineaments.

**Answer.** Lineaments

$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=35^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $35^\circ$

questions.subject: Engineering & Environmental Geology
questions.stem: Rock Quality Designation (RQD) is computed from drill core as the percentage of
options: intact pieces ≥10 cm summed over the run length
questions.solution:
**Concept.** RQD = Σ(intact pieces ≥10 cm) / total core run × 100.

**Working.**
Pieces ≥10 cm.

**Answer.** Σ pieces ≥10 cm
